series_metadata.series_description: Records in this series originate or reside in the Director''s office and include, but are not limited to, executive correspondence, memos, information, reports, and directives concerning agency operations and administration, policies, procedures, organization, and reviews, along with related records. NOTE: This series applies to records that document substantive actions, functions, policies, or procedures of the agency and may possess enduring value such that they require review by the State Archives after the required retention period has elapsed. Records that do not possess this quality may be managed with an appropriate alternative schedule such as DEV-GEN-01, DEV-GEN-02, or DEV-GEN-03 as applicable.

retention_rules.disposition: Paper: Retain for period specified for electronic format. If converted to electronic format, paper files may then be destroyed after verification of successful conversion and review by State Archives, with the exception of original documents of rare archival/historical significance which must be retained in paper form (even if converted to electronic format). Such rare documents shall be forwarded to the Legal Services office for documentation and subsequent transfer to the State Archives. ; Electronic: Retain two (2) years, then transfer to State Archives. LITIGATION HOLD: Any records subject to a litigation hold must be retained until the hold is lifted by the Legal Office, regardless of whether the specified retention period has elapsed., Paper: Archives ; Electronic: Archives
